message:
fix(ci): replace -lresolv with -lresolv.9 in zig wrappers for macOS

Zig 0.14.0 filters -lresolv as a macOS libc name and fails to find it
during cross-compilation. Using -lresolv.9 bypasses this filter and
links the versioned library correctly (ziglang/zig#16674).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Executed-By: beads/crew/emma
Rig: beads
Role: crew

files.patch:
@@ -50,10 +50,18 @@ jobs:
             "cc-x86_64-freebsd:x86_64-freebsd"; do
             name="${pair%%:*}"
             target="${pair##*:}"
-            # Serialize zig calls per target with flock to prevent
-            # AccessDenied races when go build invokes CC in parallel.
-            printf '#!/bin/sh\nexec flock /tmp/zigcc/lock-%s zig cc -target %s "$@"\n' \
-              "$target" "$target" > "/tmp/zigcc/$name"
+            # Use bash for array handling; replace -lresolv with -lresolv.9
+            # to work around zig filtering macOS libc names (ziglang/zig#16674).
+            # Serialize zig calls per target with flock to prevent races.
+            cat > "/tmp/zigcc/$name" <<'WRAPPER'
+#!/bin/bash
+args=()
+for arg in "$@"; do
+  [[ "$arg" == "-lresolv" ]] && args+=("-lresolv.9") || args+=("$arg")
+done
+WRAPPER
+            printf 'exec flock /tmp/zigcc/lock-%s zig cc -target %s "${args[@]}"\n' \
+              "$target" "$target" >> "/tmp/zigcc/$name"
             chmod +x "/tmp/zigcc/$name"
           done
